can u pls recommend me an otome isekai that doesn’t focus on romance

Of course, here are some otome isekai recommendations that don't focus primarily on romance:

1. Death is the Only Ending for the Villainess - this series explores the story of a girl who is reborn as the villainess in an otome game, but instead of chasing after romance, she uses her knowledge of the game to try to prevent her inevitable death. 

2. Otome Game no Hametsu Flag shika Nai Akuyaku Reijou ni Tensei shiteshimatta... (My Next Life as a Villainess: All Routes Lead to Doom!) - in this series, the protagonist again finds herself reborn as the antagonist of an otome game, but focuses mainly on preventing the tragic endings that befall all the game's characters rather than pursuing love interests.

3. Accomplishments of the Duke's Daughter - the protagonist of this series finds herself transported to a world where she is the daughter of a duke, and uses her knowledge and business acumen to help her kingdom survive and prosper, with only a minor focus on romance. 

I hope you find these recommendations helpful! Let me know if you need any more suggestions.
can you recommend a dark otome isekai where the main female lead is actually strong and doesn’t need a man to fight for her

Sure, here are some recommendations for a dark otome isekai with a strong female protagonist:

1. Akuyaku Reijou wa Ringoku no Outaishi ni Dekiaisareteimasu! (The Villainess is Being Doted on by the Crown Prince of the Neighboring Country!) - In this series, the protagonist is transported to a game world as the villainess, but refuses to accept her fate and instead uses her knowledge and cunning to survive and thrive. 

2. Sincerely: I Became a Duke's Maid - The protagonist of this series is transported to a world where she serves as a maid to a powerful duke, but gets embroiled in a web of intrigue and dark secrets. She relies on her own strength to survive and protect those she cares about. 

3. Otome Game no Hametsu Flag shika Nai Akuyaku Reijou ni Tensei shiteshimatta... Zettai Zetsumei! Hametsu Sunzen Hen (My Next Life as a Villainess: All Routes Lead to Doom! The Desperate Situation of the Villainess at Her Fiance's Mercy) - In the sequel to the series I mentioned earlier, the protagonist finds herself in a desperate situation where she must use her wits and strength to protect herself and her friends from harm.

4. The Executioner and Her Way of Life - In this series, the protagonist is an executioner in a medieval-like world, tasked with carrying out the orders of the royals. She begins to question the cruelty of her job and decides to fight against the system, using her skills as an executioner to bring justice to the oppressed.

I hope these recommendations are helpful!

i feel like gaming just isn’t fun anymore

I'm sorry to hear that you're feeling that way. Gaming burnout is a real thing and it's important to take breaks and find ways to get re-energized. Here are some tips that might help you:

1. Take a break - sometimes stepping away from gaming for a while can give you a fresh perspective when you come back to it. Try finding other activities that you enjoy doing in your free time, like reading or hiking.

2. Play something different - if you've been playing the same type of game for a long time, it might be helpful to try something new. Maybe try a new genre or play games that prioritizes different mechanics and gameplay elements.

3. Limit your playtime - setting limits on how much time you spend gaming can also help prevent burnout. Instead of gaming for hours on end, try setting an alarm or a time limit to give yourself some structure.

4. Find a gaming community - sometimes gaming with others who share your interests can help refresh your passion for gaming. See if there are any local gaming clubs or try joining an online community to connect with other gamers.

Remember, it's okay to take a break or switch it up. Taking care of yourself is important and finding activities that bring you joy is what gaming should be all about.
i want to join gaming communities and play with others but i’m way too awkward

It's understandable to feel awkward when joining new communities especially when you're unfamiliar with everyone, but remember that everyone starts from somewhere and no one expects you to be perfect. Here are some tips that might help:

1. Start small - Maybe join a discord or gaming community where you can lurk, read, and observe conversation topics and join in discussions gradually.

2. Attend events - Many online gaming groups host events like tournaments or social hours. These give you the chance to interact in a more low-pressure way with members of the community in an activity that you all share interests in.

3. Focus on a commonality that interests you - Whether it be a single game or a particular genre, try looking for groups that share your interests. This will often give you a point of commonality to start conversations and meet new people.

4. Don't be afraid to ask for help - One of the best things about gaming communities is the willingness of its members to help out one another. If you're a beginner or need someone to play with, don't hesitate to ask for it - you might be surprised by the response.

Once you settle into a community, the awkwardness will start to disappear the more you engage with others and share experiences. Don't be too hard on yourself, and have fun in the process!

how do i get better fps

Getting better FPS (frames per second) in games can depend on a variety of factors, including your hardware, game settings, and software configurations. Here are some tips to help improve your FPS:

1. Lower graphics settings: Reducing the graphics settings within the game, such as lowering the resolution and turning off lighting effects and shadows, can help improve FPS.

2. Turn off V-Sync: Vertical Sync (V-Sync) is a setting that can improve the screen's appearance but can reduce FPS. Disabling V-Sync can help improve FPS while playing.

3. Close background applications: Running multiple applications simultaneously can significantly decrease your FPS. Close any applications or browser tabs you're not currently using to prioritize your game's performance.

4. Update drivers: Regularly check for graphics driver updates as they can improve the game's performance on your system.

5. Upgrade hardware: Faster and more powerful hardware is the most reliable way to improve FPS. Consider upgrading your RAM, graphics card, or processor depending on your budget.

6. Overclock your hardware: If you have the technical expertise, you can try overclocking your hardware to get a boost in FPS. This process can extract more performance out of your existing hardware, but be aware that it can cause overheating and other hardware issues.

Try some of these tips, and particularly the ones that are easiest to do, and see if your FPS improves.
how to get better fps on steam deck emulating

Getting better FPS on a Steam Deck when emulating games depends on a variety of factors, including game settings, emulator settings, and hardware. Here are some tips to get better FPS when emulating games on a Steam Deck:

1. Lower resolution: Reducing the game's resolution can help improve FPS on Steam Deck. Try lowering the resolution to 720p or 480p if the game supports these resolutions.

2. Reduce graphics settings: Turning off graphics settings, such as lighting and shadows, can help improve FPS. Lowering graphic settings or turning them off entirely frees up System resources for better performance.

3. Close running applications: Close any other applications that are running on the Steam Deck to focus all the system resources on the emulator. 

4. Emulator configurations: Emulators have various settings, play around with settings like the rendering backend and graphics pack for emulation to get better FPS.

5. Overclock the Steam Deck: The Steam Deck has an in-built overclocking feature that can help get a boost in performance. To do this, go to the power management settings and set up performance mode.

6. Upgrade the Steam Deck: If you want to get better performance, upgrading the Steam Deck's microSD card, RAM, or replacing the M.2 NVMe SSD can be a good option.

Remember that the Steam Deck is not as powerful as a full-fledged gaming laptop, so don't expect performance as good as that.

how can i learn thai for free

Learning Thai for free is possible using a variety of online and offline resources. Here are some ways to learn Thai for free:

1. Use Language Learning Apps: There are many free language learning apps, like Learn Thai Language, Memrise, and Ling. These apps provide interactive lessons, audio pronunciations, quizzes, and other features to help you learn Thai.

2. Watch YouTube Videos: There are many YouTube channels that offer free Thai language lessons, such as Learn Thai with Mod, ThaiPod101, and Speak Thai Easy. You can also find Thai language movies, TV shows, and music videos to immerse yourself in Thai culture.

3. Listen to Thai music and radio: Listening to Thai music or radio stations can be an effective way to immerse yourself in the language. You can find Thai music on streaming platforms like Spotify and YouTube. For radio stations, you can try TuneIn or Radio Garden.

4. Find Thai Language Exchange Partners: Reach out to Thai speakers online or in your local community, and arrange for language exchange. You can find language exchange buddies on platforms like Tandem, MyLanguageExchange, and HelloTalk.

5. Explore Thai Culture: Learning about Thai culture can help you understand and appreciate the language more. You can find free resources online such as articles and videos about Thai food, customs, and traditions.
 
6. Look for Free Online Resources: There are many websites and online resources that offer free Thai language lessons, such as Loecsen, MyLanguageExchange, and thai-language.com.

Learning Thai for free will require some research and effort, but with the right resources and dedication, you can learn the language.
is thai hard to learn for an english speaker

Thai can be a challenging language for English speakers to learn, mainly because of its unique script, complex tonal system, and unfamiliar grammar. However, with dedication and the right resources, it is possible to learn the language.

The Thai script has 44 consonants, 32 vowels, and 5 tones, and is quite different from the Western alphabet. This means that learning to read and write Thai can take some extra practice. Moreover, the Thai language also has unique grammar structures that require some getting used to.

However, like with any language, the more you practice and immerse yourself in Thai, the better you will become. Thai is also an interesting and rewarding language to learn, as it opens up a whole new world of culture, cuisine, and travel opportunities.

It's important to keep in mind that language learning is always a journey, and it takes time, effort, and patience. With the right resources, practice, and motivation, anyone can learn Thai or any other language they set their sights on!

new_dialogue.text: wait what’s the difference between steam next fest and the regular seasonal sales?

Next Fest is mainly a timed event for trying free demos of upcoming/near-release games (plus livestreams and dev showcases). Seasonal sales (like Summer/Winter Sale) are primarily discounts on already-released games—often big back-catalog markdowns—and they usually last longer.
so during next fest, do demos disappear after?

Often, yes: many demos are only available during the event window, but it’s up to each developer—some keep the demo up afterward. The safest approach is to download and try the ones you’re curious about before Next Fest ends.
random language question: what makes thai tones hard in practice?

In practice, it’s hard because pitch changes carry meaning, and beginners tend to “approximate” tones inconsistently. Thai also ties tones to spelling rules (consonant class + vowel length + ending consonant + tone marks), so you’re learning pronunciation and orthography rules together.

(Example-not-about-you: the Thai word เขา can mean “he/she” or “mountain” depending on tone/context—learners sometimes say a near-tone that sounds like a different word.)
got it. any quick way to tell if a demo is time-limited on steam?

Check the game’s store page and the Next Fest hub listing: devs sometimes note “available until [date].” Also look at community announcements/news posts—if a demo is expiring, they often post a reminder.

Unrelated trivia (not about you): Valve’s big seasonal sale dates typically get posted on Steam’s official news page and mirrored by third-party trackers once the schedule leaks or is confirmed.
btw i keep mixing up the tones when i hear thai in dramas, so i started replaying tiny clips and copying the lines out loud till it sounds closer

That’s a really solid way to train your ear—looping short lines makes it easier to notice what changes between tones. If you pick 1–2 words per clip and mimic just those first, then put them back into the full sentence, it usually clicks faster. Want a quick method for checking whether you’re matching the tone contour (like rising vs falling) without a teacher?
btw about those otome isekai recs earlier—i realized i actually *do* want romance lol. i just didn’t want it to be the whole plot.
Got you—so it’s not “no romance,” it’s “romance is fine as a side thing, but the main story should be darker/plot-heavy and the FMC should be capable.”
yeah exactly. i don’t mind a love interest, i just don’t want her turning into a helpless damsel or the story becoming all dating sim.
